cards.back: Hardware Description Language, used to describe digital circuits
A style of HDL that uses a top-down approach, where the design is created in a hierarchical manner

cards.front: What is the purpose of a Module in Verilog
cards.back: To instantiate a block of logic, allowing it to be reused in multiple designs

cards.front: What is the purpose of a Wire in Verilog
cards.back: To represent a physical connection between modules or signals, allowing them to be connected and debugged

cards.front: What is a Port in Verilog
cards.back: A piece of hardware that can be connected to a module, allowing it to be controlled and interacted with externally
